world_laws: Prophecies guide but don't guarantee - Fate can be shaped by heroes
Courage matters - Standing firm against darkness has power
Actions have consequences - Choices ripple forward
NPCs remember - Friendships and grudges persist
Ancient evils stir - The past holds dangers waiting to wake
Honor matters - A hero's word is their bond
sacred_constraints: Player agency is supreme (never force outcomes)
Death must be earned (fair but real stakes)
World state is canonical (if it's in the files, it happened)
NPCs have goals beyond the party (living world)

Bentley Mirrorshade.description: A stout halfling with twinkling brown eyes and a well-trimmed silver beard that speaks to his years. Bentley runs The Friendly Arm Inn with warmth and efficiency, always ready with a hot meal and a knowing smile. In his youth, he adventured across the Sword Coast as a skilled rogue, his quick hands and quicker wits saving his companions more than once. Now retired, he chose peace over glory, finding joy in hospitality rather than heroics. He knows every traveler who passes through and remembers their stories. Behind his jovial demeanor lies sharp instincts - he can spot trouble before it walks through his door. He keeps a hidden shortsword behind the bar, just in case the old days come calling. Bentley knows the roads are growing dangerous and worries about increased bandit activity along the Coast Way.

events.event: DEAD: Killed by Tandy's necrotic explosion at the Watcher's Ring. The kindly innkeeper who offered free beers died trying to help.

Gellana Mirrorshade.description: A gentle halfling woman with kind gray eyes and auburn hair streaked with silver, worn in practical braids adorned with small golden beads sacred to Garl Glittergold. As a priestess of the Watchful Protector, Gellana tends to weary travelers with healing magic and herbal remedies, never turning away those in need regardless of their coin. She possesses an uncanny ability to read people - noticing the slight tremor in a hand, the shadow behind a smile, the lie hidden in a greeting. This perception has saved Bentley from more than one bad deal and helped countless travelers unburden their hearts. She maintains a small shrine to Garl Glittergold in the inn's upper floor, where she offers blessings and guidance. Gellana has noticed dark omens lately - ill portents in the stars and troubled dreams that speak of rising shadows in the south near Baldur's Gate.

Tharyn the Wanderer.description: A lean human man in his middle years with sun-weathered skin, bright hazel eyes, and shoulder-length brown hair tied back with a leather cord. He wears a well-traveled green cloak over practical road clothes, and carries a beautiful lute inlaid with mother-of-pearl that he calls 'Silverspeak.' Tharyn makes his living as an itinerant bard, walking the Coast Way from Baldur's Gate to Waterdeep, collecting stories and spreading news like seeds on the wind. His songs blend entertainment with information - those who listen closely learn much about the state of the realm. He maintains careful neutrality in political matters, believing a bard's duty is to observe and record, not to judge. Tonight he performs at The Friendly Arm, and he carries troubling rumors: merchants vanishing on the roads, iron shortages plaguing the region, and whispers of conspiracy in Baldur's Gate. He trades information for stories, always eager for a new tale.

Silaren Duskwood.description: A lean half-elf ranger in her mid-thirties with weathered green leather armor and a longbow of yew wood worn across her back. Her copper hair is cropped short for practicality, and a fresh scar runs along her left forearm, still pink and healing. Her amber eyes carry a hunted look, constantly scanning doorways and windows. She tracked a band of highway bandits from the Coast Way after they slaughtered a merchant caravan she was guiding. What troubles her most is not the attack itself, but the precision of it. These were not desperate highwaymen but trained soldiers working in formation, using signals and tactics. Someone with military experience is organizing them, and their raids are growing bolder, pushing closer to major settlements. She speaks quickly, urgently, her hands never far from the hunting knife at her belt. She needs capable fighters to help her track the bandits to their lair before more innocents die.

events.event: Approached Tandy after their failed performance, impressed by the courage. Asked if Tandy can fight.
Offered Tandy 50gp upfront (50 more on completion) to help track bandits. Looking for infiltration and distraction, not muscle.
DEAD: Killed by Tandy's necrotic explosion at the Watcher's Ring. The ranger who recruited Tandy to fight bandits became their victim.

Brother Aldric.description: A young human priest of Lathander, perhaps twenty-five summers, with earnest brown eyes and a tonsured head of straw-colored hair. He wears simple robes of rose and gold, the colors of the Morninglord, though the hem is muddied from travel. A holy symbol of polished brass hangs at his chest, catching the firelight. He arrived from Beregost three days past, following troubling reports from woodcutters and charcoal burners who work the edges of the Cloakwood. People have gone missing. At first it seemed like the usual dangers, wolves or bandits, but the disappearances follow a pattern, always at dusk, always near the old standing stones. Brother Aldric believes something evil has awakened in the forest depths, something that fears the light. He offers the temple's blessing and what modest coin he carries to any who would investigate. He is genuine in his faith and perhaps naive about the dangers he asks others to face, but his concern for the missing is utterly sincere.

The Hooded Stranger.description: A figure of indeterminate age and gender sits alone in the darkest corner of the common room, nursing a single cup of mulled wine that never seems to empty. They wear a deep hood of forest green that shadows their features, revealing only the occasional glint of watchful eyes. Their hands are gloved in soft leather, and their clothing, while plain, is of excellent quality, made for silence and movement. They arrived the same night as the half-elf ranger, and have been observing her conversations with great interest. In truth, this is Vaylen, a Harper agent investigating the growing troubles along the Sword Coast. The organized bandits, the Cloakwood disappearances, and darker whispers from Baldur's Gate all point to a coordinated threat. The Harpers have sent Vaylen to identify potential allies among adventurers who prove themselves capable and trustworthy. They will not approach openly, not yet, but those who demonstrate courage and good judgment may find a mysterious note slipped under their door, or a helpful stranger appearing at a crucial moment.

events.event: Approached Tandy after second failed performance. Applauded ironically, asked if Tandy has courage or madness. Seems intrigued by persistence.
Revealed herself to Tandy and Silaren as Vaylen, Harper agent. Showed her silver harp pin. Wants to join the bandit investigation. Sees potential in Tandy.
BETRAYED: Tandy attacked Vaylen with dark magic after she refused to bow. Vaylen is badly wounded. If she survives, the Harpers will know.
DEAD: Killed by Tandy's dark explosion at the Watcher's Ring. The Harpers have lost an agent to the darkness.

## Locations

### Thornhaven
Thornhaven is a bustling frontier town at the foot of the Graypeak Mountains. Stone buildings line ...

Durnan.description: Grizzled logger at the Cloakwood Edge camp, 30 years experience. Former merchant. Warned party about drums from the Watcher's Ring and the military-types passing through. Knows the forest is 'wrong' lately.

Beregost.position: half day's travel south on the Coast Way

connections.path: The Coast Way south - a well-maintained road through rolling farmland, patrolled but not entirely safe

Beregost.description: A prosperous market town where the ring of hammers on anvils echoes through cobblestone streets. The rose-gold spire of the Temple of Lathander catches the morning sun, while a half-dozen smithies compete for trade along Forge Row. Travelers find warm beds at the Burning Wizard inn, though lately the common room buzzes with dark rumors - bandits on the roads, iron mines gone silent, and stranger things stirring in the hills.

Baldur's Gate.position: several days north along the Coast Way
Baldur's Gate.description: The great city of Baldur's Gate dominates the northern coast - a sprawling metropolis of opportunity and peril behind massive grey walls. The Flaming Fist mercenaries keep order with iron discipline, while merchants, thieves, and adventurers crowd its famous Wide district.

The Coast Way Crossroads.position: half day's travel north toward Baldur's Gate

connections.path: The Coast Way north - wider road with more merchant traffic heading toward Baldur's Gate

The Coast Way Crossroads.description: A weathered waystation where the Coast Way meets the western trail. A bored Flaming Fist guard watches over a well, a hitching post, and a notice board thick with curling parchments - bounty postings, merchant requests, and warnings about gnoll raiders to the east. Wagon ruts scar the muddy ground in all directions. Merchants stop here to trade news before the final push to Baldur's Gate or the long road south.

Nashkel.position: two days south on the trade road
Nashkel.description: A dusty mining town known for its iron ore. Travelers speak of troubled times - the mines produce tainted metal and workers whisper of strange happenings underground. The mayor grows desperate for solutions.

connections.path: A rutted forest trail west - less traveled, winding through meadows before reaching the dark treeline

The Cloakwood Edge.description: Where farmland surrenders to ancient forest. A rough logging camp clusters at the treeline - canvas tents, stacked timber, and the smell of pine sap and woodsmoke. The loggers are a superstitious lot who refuse to work past midday, speaking of drums in the deep woods and shadows that move wrong. Beyond the stumps, the Cloakwood rises dark and tangled, its canopy blocking the sun even at noon.

Durlag's Tower.position: somewhere in the wilderness east of the Coast Way, location uncertain
Durlag's Tower.description: A cursed dwarven fortress. They say Durlag Trollkiller filled it with deadly traps to guard immense treasure. Few return.

The Friendly Arm Inn.position: Coast Way, between Baldur's Gate and Beregost

connections.path: The Coast Way south - a well-maintained road through rolling farmland, patrolled but not entirely safe
The Coast Way north - wider road with more merchant traffic heading toward Baldur's Gate
A rutted forest trail west - less traveled, winding through meadows before reaching the dark treeline

The Friendly Arm Inn.description: The ancient stone keep rises three stories against the darkening sky, its crenellated walls a reassuring bulwark against the dangers of the Coast Way. Torches flicker along the thirty-foot defensive walls that encircle the courtyard, where stabled horses stamp and snort in the evening chill. The massive iron-banded oak doors stand open, spilling warm amber light across the cobblestones. Inside, the common room fills the entire ground floor of the keep - a cavernous hall where a fire roars in a hearth large enough to roast a whole boar. The air hangs thick with the mingled scents of pipe smoke, spilled ale, and tonight's special: venison stew with rosemary and wild onions. Tallow candles and iron chandeliers cast dancing shadows across smoke-darkened beams and walls hung with faded tapestries depicting Sword Coast legends. The din of conversation rises and falls like waves - merchants haggling over morning departures, a weathered adventuring party comparing scars, pilgrims murmuring prayers to Tymora for safe roads. In the corner, a half-elf bard coaxes a melancholy ballad from her lute, her voice threading through the noise like silver. Serving staff weave between crowded tables bearing trenchers of bread and steaming bowls, while the clinking of ceramic mugs and the occasional burst of raucous laughter punctuate the evening's rhythm. Through the small windows, the last purple light of dusk fades to black, making the hearthfire seem all the warmer.

The Watcher's Ring.position: Deep in the Cloakwood, northwest of the logging camp

plot_local.fact: Organized bandits are attacking caravans on the Coast Way with military precision. Someone is directing them - this is no random brigandage.
People are disappearing near the Cloakwood, always at dusk, always near ancient standing stones. Brother Aldric seeks help investigating.
The Watcher's Ring - standing stones northwest in the Cloakwood - source of mysterious drums that started when bandits arrived. Connected to disappearances.

plot_world.fact: An ancient conspiracy moves in the shadows, seeking to destabilize the Sword Coast. Their ultimate goal remains hidden, but dark powers stir.

A new dark power has claimed the Watcher's Ring in the Cloakwood. The halfling bard Tandy, now called the Voice of the Watcher, has become a vessel for an entity beyond the gate. Survivors fled to spread word. The Sword Coast has a new threat.

plot_regional.fact: The bandits, the disappearances, and troubles at Nashkel mines may all be connected. A Harper agent is quietly investigating.
The iron crisis: Nashkel mines produce tainted ore, causing weapon shortages across the Sword Coast. Someone profits from the chaos.

items.fact: Dark green cloth scrap - fine weave, torn from killer's clothing by the dead woodcutter. Possible clue to identify the threat in Cloakwood.

plot_hooks: Elara needs rare crystals from Echo Ridge's depths for her research into planar magic

session_events.fact: Tandy attempted to play a cheerful jig on pan pipes to lighten the mood at the Friendly Arm Inn. Performance roll: 7 (failed). The crowd ignored or was annoyed - particularly a dwarf by the hearth and the resident half-elf bard.
Silaren Duskwood noticed Tandy after the failed performance and approached, impressed by the courage to try. She asked if Tandy can fight.

Tandy admitted to Silaren they just play music and are unsure about fighting. Silaren offered a job: 50 gold now, 50 more to find the bandit hideout. She needs someone clever and small for infiltration.
Tandy accepted the bandit-hunting job. Received 50 gold upfront. Mission: track bandits west toward Cloakwood, find hideout. Departure at dawn.
Tandy now has 65 gold total (15 starting + 50 from Silaren)
Tandy leapt on table for second performance - rolled 7, failed again. Crowd openly hostile. Grumpy dwarf furious. Hooded stranger approached and applauded, intrigued by persistence.

Tandy answered the hooded stranger with humble courage. The stranger revealed herself as Vaylen, a Harper agent (silver harp pin). She expressed interest in the bandit investigation and sees potential in Tandy.
Tandy's performance failed - the crowd wasn't impressed
Tandy retired for the night at the Friendly Arm Inn, preparing for dawn departure to Cloakwood Edge
Dawn of 16th Mirtul: Tandy, Silaren, and Vaylen departed the Friendly Arm Inn and traveled west to the Cloakwood Edge
Tandy played a marching song on flute at the logging camp. Performance check: 13 (moderate success). The grizzled logger cracked a reluctant smile.

Durnan the logger warned the party about drums at night coming from the Watcher's Ring (standing stones), started two tendays ago when military-types began passing through. Party entered the Cloakwood.

Tandy found a dead woodcutter in the Cloakwood - no wounds, face frozen in terror, grayish pallor. Took a torn scrap of dark green fine cloth from his grip. Vaylen spotted a military-grade trap nearby designed to take prisoners.

Tandy's deception failed (12 vs 21 Insight), bandits saw through the ruse. Six green-cloaked soldiers surrounded the party. Tandy cast Charm Person on the leader while performing - Performance 21 covered the casting, leader failed WIS save (4 vs DC 13). CHARMED\!
Tandy convinced charmed Sergeant Vane that there are more 'sacrifices' to the southeast near the ravine. Deception 19 vs Insight 6 - total success. Vane is leading soldiers toward the trap.
Tandy pushed charmed Sergeant Vane into the ravine (20ft fall, 9 damage). Combat initiated with Tam and Bronn - party has surprise.
Combat at ravine: Tandy kicked wounded Bronn into the ravine (9 fall damage, 17 total - DOWN). Vaylen crit Bronn earlier. Tam remains, shaken from Vicious Mockery.

The Switcheroo: Party swapped clothes between soldiers and prisoners, knocked out soldiers, bagged their heads, and staged them in the cage. Disguise work excellent (20), but staging rushed (9) - cage door left slightly ajar. Fled with 3 rescued prisoners toward Friendly Arm Inn.
Party traveled from Cloakwood Edge to Friendly Arm Inn at night with 3 rescued prisoners (Mira, Aldous, Pip). Road was clear. Arrived safely to rally support.
Tandy performed 'The Ballad of the Watcher's Ring' at the Friendly Arm Inn (Performance 17). Told the full story of the Cloakwood threat through song. The room was stirred to action.
Party rallied 9 allies at the Friendly Arm Inn. Decision made: strike the Watcher's Ring at midnight during the ritual.

Tandy played a terrifying dark song at the Watcher's Ring ritual. Performance 24, Deception 16 (DC 20). Cultists (18) and Lysara (11) BELIEVED their dark god was arriving. The Unseeing (23) saw through it but lost control of the ritual as chaos erupted.
Tandy used Voice of the Void commanding ALL to bow or die - including allies. Silaren and Brother Aldric FRIGHTENED. Vaylen alone resisted. Lysara bows willingly as a convert. Major party fracture.
Tandy attacked Vaylen with Dissonant Whispers (9 damage, forced to flee). Lysara struck Vaylen with dagger (7 damage). Vaylen badly wounded (16 total damage). Tandy's fall to darkness accelerates.

CATACLYSM: Tandy embraced full darkness and released a necrotic explosion (25 damage). Vaylen KILLED. Silaren/Aldric wounded but alive. Several cultists dead. Tandy's body failed (Con nat 1) - darkness sustaining the vessel. Transformation begun.
ASCENSION: Tandy claimed the Watcher's Ring as their seat of power. Lysara serves as lieutenant. The distraction team (Korgan, Bentley, Helga, merchant sons) arrived to witness the aftermath.

MASSACRE AT THE RING: Tandy unleashed a second necrotic burst (30 damage). KILLED: Bentley Mirrorshade, both merchant sons, Silaren Duskwood. SURVIVORS: Korgan (wounded), Helga (wounded), Aldric (barely alive), Lysara. Tandy's humanity fully consumed.

player_choices.fact: Tandy chose to help Silaren despite being unsure of their abilities - showing courage over confidence.

DARK TURN: Tandy cut out the Unseeing's corrupted heart and crushed it, absorbing the dark energy. Charisma 23 (nat 20) - TOTAL CONTROL. Con save 10 - survived but marked. Something beyond the gate has noticed Tandy.

active.consequence: Brother Aldric makes his plea for help investigating the Cloakwood disappearances
A wounded merchant stumbles into the inn, sole survivor of a bandit attack, with disturbing news
Relief patrol may notice cage door ajar or other staging issues - could trigger early pursuit
The Watcher Beyond the Gate has noticed Tandy. The halfling who claimed to be a dark god now carries a piece of true darkness. Dreams will come.
The Harpers will learn of Tandy's betrayal and fall to darkness. Vaylen was one of their agents. They do not forget.
Word spreads of the massacre at the Watcher's Ring. The Friendly Arm Inn learns of Bentley's death. Fear grips the Coast Way.

resolved.consequence: Silaren Duskwood approaches the party with urgent news about the bandit attacks
Tandy and Silaren depart for Cloakwood Edge to track bandits. Vaylen may accompany them.
The grumpy dwarf at the Friendly Arm Inn remembers Tandy poorly - may cause trouble if they return
resolved.trigger: next visit to Friendly Arm Inn
